equipmentCategories.title: Brewhouses
equipmentCategories.h1: New and Used Brewhouses For Sale
equipmentCategories.metaTitle: New and Used Brewhouses For Sale | Beverage Equipment Traders — San Marcos, CA
equipmentCategories.metaDescription: Shop new and used brewhouses from 7BBL to 30BBL+. Photographed, documented, and ready to ship nationwide from San Marcos, CA. Rigging and installation available.
equipmentCategories.description: Beverage Equipment Traders maintains one of the largest inventories of new and used brewhouses in the western United States. Every system is photographed and listed with condition documentation.
equipmentCategories.bodyContent:
Complete brewhouse systems from 7BBL to 30BBL and larger, sourced from working brewery liquidations, upgrades, and direct manufacturer relationships. Our catalog spans 2-vessel and 3-vessel configurations from manufacturers including Marks Design and Metalworks, Specific Mechanical Systems, Premier Stainless, and DME Brewing.

What sets Beverage Equipment Traders apart is operator context. We started as a brewery. We have sized brewhouses, run the same equipment we sell, and troubleshot every failure point buyers encounter. When you call us, you are speaking with someone who has actually brewed on it.

Every brewhouse in our inventory is photographed inside and out. High-value systems include inspection documentation, manufacturer details, and condition notes beyond a single-word summary. For full brewhouse systems, we also provide rigging, de-installation, transportation, and installation nationwide.

Whether you are scaling a production facility, outfitting a new build, or sourcing a specific vessel configuration, our inventory is updated regularly and our team is available to help match equipment to your capacity needs.

faqs.q: What brewhouse sizes do you carry?
faqs.a: Our inventory includes systems from 7BBL pilot setups through 30BBL+ production brewhouses. Availability changes frequently — contact us for current stock.

faqs.q: Do you offer rigging and installation for brewhouses?
faqs.a: Yes. Beverage Equipment Traders provides full-service rigging, de-installation, transportation, and installation for all brewhouse systems we sell. We ship and install nationwide.

faqs.q: Can I inspect a brewhouse before purchasing?
faqs.a: Yes. Our facility in San Marcos, CA is open for in-person inspection by appointment. We strongly encourage buyers to inspect high-value systems prior to purchase.

faqs.q: What manufacturers do you typically carry?
faqs.a: Our brewhouse inventory includes systems from Marks Design and Metalworks, Specific Mechanical Systems, Premier Stainless, DME Brewing, and others sourced from working brewery liquidations and upgrades.

faqs.q: Can you help me sell my current brewhouse when I upgrade?
faqs.a: Yes. We handle consignment and direct purchase of outgoing brewhouses when you upgrade. Many buyers run both transactions through us — sourcing the new system and liquidating the old one in a single coordinated process.

buyerGuide.title: Define Your Capacity
buyerGuide.description: Start with your projected weekly batch volume and work backward to barrel size. Buying for current volume is the most common mistake — size for 18-month growth.

buyerGuide.title: Confirm Vessel Configuration
buyerGuide.description: 2-vessel vs 3-vessel determines your brew day efficiency and lautering capability. Direct fire vs steam heating affects your utility infrastructure requirements.

buyerGuide.title: Review Condition Documentation
buyerGuide.description: Our listings include condition notes, manufacturer data, and photography. For systems over $30K, in-person inspection at our San Marcos facility is strongly recommended.

buyerGuide.title: Get a Rigging Quote
buyerGuide.description: Before committing, get a full rigging and installation quote. We provide a single price covering de-installation, freight, delivery, set, level, and commissioning.

buyerGuide.title: Close and Commission
buyerGuide.description: Our crew handles delivery and installation. We do not leave until the system runs. Every brewhouse installation includes a commissioning walkthrough with your team.

bestFitBuyers.title: New Brewery Build
bestFitBuyers.description: Starting from scratch and need a production-ready system at below-new cost? Used brewhouses from working facilities offer the best value per barrel of capacity.

bestFitBuyers.title: Capacity Expansion
bestFitBuyers.description: Adding a second brewhouse to an existing facility? We can match systems to your utility infrastructure and existing cellar layout.

bestFitBuyers.title: Brewery Upgrade
bestFitBuyers.description: Replacing a smaller system with a larger one? We handle consignment of your outgoing brewhouse and sourcing of the replacement — one transaction.

bestFitBuyers.title: Contract Brewing
bestFitBuyers.description: Building out contract brewing capacity and need a production-scale system quickly? Our inventory turns over regularly with production-proven systems ready to ship.

equipmentCategories.title: Fermenters & Tanks
equipmentCategories.h1: Used Fermenters and Fermentation Tanks For Sale
equipmentCategories.metaTitle: Used Fermenters & Fermentation Tanks For Sale | Beverage Equipment Traders
equipmentCategories.metaDescription: New and used fermenters, unitanks, brite tanks, and fermentation vessels. Multiple sizes and manufacturers in stock. Nationwide shipping from San Marcos, CA.
equipmentCategories.description: Our fermenter inventory spans conical fermenters, jacketed unitanks, and brite tanks across a wide range of volumes from multiple manufacturers.
equipmentCategories.bodyContent:
Fermenters are among the most searched pieces of brewery capital equipment, and our inventory reflects genuine depth. Conical fermenters, jacketed unitanks, brite tanks, and open fermentation vessels across volumes from pilot-scale to full production. Manufacturers represented include JV Northwest, Premier Stainless, Specific Mechanical, and others.

Key specs buyers evaluate include vessel volume, cone angle, CIP compatibility, jacket configuration, stainless grade (304 vs 316L), and inlet/outlet sizing. Our listings include specification data alongside condition notes and photography. For unitanks handling both fermentation and conditioning, we note tank history where available.

Brite tanks in our inventory are typically sourced from production breweries upgrading volume, meaning they arrive with documented brewing history, legitimate usage context, and often at significantly below-replacement cost.

All fermentation vessels can be shipped nationwide. Rigging and installation services are available for multi-vessel moves and facility builds. Contact us for current availability and lead times.

faqs.q: What fermenter sizes do you typically carry?
faqs.a: We carry fermenters from small pilot sizes up to large production vessels. Inventory changes regularly — reach out for current availability on specific volumes.

faqs.q: Do you sell brite tanks separately from fermenters?
faqs.a: Yes. Brite tanks, unitanks, and conical fermenters are all available independently. We can also help source matched sets if you are building out a cellaring program.

faqs.q: What stainless grades do your fermenters typically come in?
faqs.a: Most production brewery fermenters are 304 stainless. 316L vessels appear in our inventory and are noted in listings. If you require 316L for your application, contact us and we will source accordingly.

faqs.q: Can you source matched sets of fermenters for a new cellar build?
faqs.a: Yes. For new cellar builds we source matched vessel sets — same manufacturer, same volume, same jacket configuration — at significantly below-new cost. Contact us with your cellar spec and we will advise on current availability.

faqs.q: Do you carry fermenters for distillery or cidery applications?
faqs.a: Yes. Standard conical fermenters and jacketed tanks from brewery production are appropriate for distillery wash fermentation and cider production. The same inventory serves all fermentation applications.

buyerGuide.title: Calculate Cellar Volume
buyerGuide.description: Match fermenter volume to your brewhouse output and desired fermentation time. A 15 BBL brewhouse typically needs 3-4x fermentation capacity to maintain steady production.

buyerGuide.title: Choose Vessel Type
buyerGuide.description: Unitanks ferment and condition in one vessel. Dedicated fermenters plus brite tanks give more flexibility but require more space. Confirm your cellar layout before purchasing.

buyerGuide.title: Review Specifications
buyerGuide.description: Confirm cone angle, jacket configuration, CIP spray ball positioning, and inlet/outlet sizes match your existing plumbing and glycol system.

buyerGuide.title: Confirm Glycol Compatibility
buyerGuide.description: Verify your glycol chiller has sufficient capacity for the additional vessels. Adding fermentation volume without increasing chilling capacity creates production bottlenecks.

buyerGuide.title: Schedule Delivery
buyerGuide.description: Multi-vessel moves require coordinated rigging. We handle delivery, set, level, and CIP connection as part of our installation service.

bestFitBuyers.title: Expanding Cellar Capacity
bestFitBuyers.description: Adding fermentation volume to keep up with brewhouse output? We source matched vessels to your existing cellar configuration.

bestFitBuyers.title: Building a New Cellar
bestFitBuyers.description: Outfitting a new facility from scratch? We can source a full matched set of fermenters and brite tanks at significantly below-new cost.

bestFitBuyers.title: Upgrading to Larger Vessels
bestFitBuyers.description: Moving from smaller fermenters to production-scale vessels as you scale? We handle consignment of outgoing tanks and sourcing of replacements.

bestFitBuyers.title: Cidery or Distillery Build
bestFitBuyers.description: Fermentation vessels for cider, spirits, kombucha, and beverage production require the same stainless equipment. Our inventory serves all these applications.

equipmentCategories.title: Canning & Packaging
equipmentCategories.h1: Canning Equipment and Canning Lines For Sale
equipmentCategories.metaTitle: Canning Equipment & Canning Lines For Sale | Beverage Equipment Traders
equipmentCategories.metaDescription: Used and new canning machines, crowlers, shrink sleeve applicators, and complete canning lines. Ships nationwide from San Marcos, CA.
equipmentCategories.description: Commercial canning machines, crowler fillers, Wild Goose canning lines, and full packaging systems for craft producers across all volume tiers.
equipmentCategories.bodyContent:
Canning and packaging equipment for craft beverage producers at every scale. Our inventory covers everything from crowler machines for taproom-volume operations through full automated canning lines capable of 50+ CPM. Wild Goose canning systems, manual and semi-automatic crowlers, shrink sleeve applicators, and inline date coders.

Canning line selection depends on your production volume, package format, and how the line integrates with your existing cellar. The most common mistake buyers make is under-sizing — selecting a line to current volume rather than projected growth. We are happy to walk through what your operation actually needs.

Shrink sleeve applicators and labeling equipment also available. The TriPack LSA 160 and similar mid-volume systems are suited for production lines running 20 to 100+ cases per hour, appropriate for craft producers and non-alcoholic beverage facilities.

All equipment ships nationwide. Rigging, installation, and commissioning services available. Contact us to confirm current canning inventory and lead times.

faqs.q: What is the difference between a crowler machine and a full canning line?
faqs.a: A crowler machine seals individual crowler cans at taproom volumes — typically 5–20 cans per minute. A full canning line is designed for packaging production runs, with fill speeds of 30–120+ CPM and inline seaming, date coding, and sometimes shrink sleeves.

faqs.q: Do you carry Wild Goose canning lines?
faqs.a: Yes, Wild Goose systems appear in our inventory regularly. Contact us for current availability on specific models.

faqs.q: Can you help with installation and commissioning of a canning line?
faqs.a: Yes. Our rigging team handles delivery, installation, utility connections, and commissioning runs for all canning line purchases. We do not leave until the line runs clean.

faqs.q: Do you carry shrink sleeve applicators and labeling equipment?
faqs.a: Yes. Shrink sleeve applicators and inline labeling systems appear in our inventory alongside canning lines. The TriPack LSA 160 and similar mid-volume systems are available for craft and beverage production lines.

faqs.q: What is the process for buying a used canning line from BET?
faqs.a: Contact our trade desk with your required CPM, can format, and budget. We advise on current inventory, provide condition documentation, and quote rigging and installation as a bundled scope if needed.

buyerGuide.title: Determine Required Speed
buyerGuide.description: Calculate cans per minute needed to package your weekly volume in your available packaging days. This is the single most important spec when selecting a line.

buyerGuide.title: Confirm Can Format
buyerGuide.description: Standard 12oz, 16oz, and 19.2oz formats are most common. Confirm the line supports your can format and whether it can be converted if your format changes.

buyerGuide.title: Evaluate Integration
buyerGuide.description: How does the line connect to your bright tank? What conveyance, depalletizer, and date coding do you need? Full-line integration planning prevents costly post-purchase additions.

buyerGuide.title: Review Condition and History
buyerGuide.description: Canning lines have high-wear components — seamer heads, fill valves, conveyor belts. Our condition documentation covers operational status and known wear items.

buyerGuide.title: Plan Installation
buyerGuide.description: Canning line installation requires floor space planning, utility connections, and often electrical work. Our team coordinates full installation including commissioning runs.

bestFitBuyers.title: Entering Can Packaging
bestFitBuyers.description: Moving from draft-only to packaged product? A used semi-auto line at 20-30 CPM is the right starting point for most craft operations entering cans.

bestFitBuyers.title: Scaling Packaging Volume
bestFitBuyers.description: Your current line is the bottleneck. Upgrading to a higher-speed system to match brewhouse and fermentation output.

bestFitBuyers.title: Taproom Crowler Program
bestFitBuyers.description: Adding a crowler program to your taproom? Semi-auto and manual crowler machines provide a low-cost entry point for on-premise can sales.

bestFitBuyers.title: Contract Packaging
bestFitBuyers.description: Building a contract packaging operation? Production-scale lines at used prices make the economics of contract canning viable at lower contract volumes.

equipmentCategories.title: Glycol Chillers
equipmentCategories.h1: Glycol Chillers and Cooling Systems For Brewing
equipmentCategories.metaTitle: Glycol Chillers & Cooling Systems For Brewing | Beverage Equipment Traders
equipmentCategories.metaDescription: New and used glycol chillers for brewing. G&D Chillers, Pro Refrigeration, and more. In stock in San Marcos, CA. Ships nationwide.
equipmentCategories.description: Glycol chillers and complete cooling systems from G&D Chillers, Pro Refrigeration, and other leading manufacturers, sized for production breweries.
equipmentCategories.bodyContent:
Glycol chilling systems are critical infrastructure for any fermentation program. We carry chillers from G&D Chillers, Pro Refrigeration, Glycool, and other manufacturers in tonnage ranges suited for both smaller craft operations and large production facilities.

Chiller sizing depends on total fermentation volume, cellar design, ambient facility temperatures, and whether the system is serving fermenters only or also cold-side packaging equipment. Undersizing a chiller creates production bottlenecks that are expensive to correct post-installation. We can help buyers evaluate sizing requirements before purchase.

Used chillers from working breweries are a value-strong option for producers who need proven, heavy-duty cooling capacity at significantly below-replacement cost. Our used chillers are photographed and listed with condition documentation.

Contact us for current glycol chiller inventory. We can also source specific tonnage ranges on request.

faqs.q: How do I know what size glycol chiller I need?
faqs.a: Sizing depends on total fermentation volume, cellar vessel count, ambient temperature, and whether you are cooling packaging equipment as well. Our team can help you work through the calculation before you purchase.

faqs.q: Do you carry G&D and Pro Refrigeration units?
faqs.a: Yes, both brands appear in our inventory regularly. Contact us for current availability.

faqs.q: Can glycol chillers be used for distillery or cidery fermentation?
faqs.a: Yes. Glycol chilling applies equally to distillery wash fermentation and cider production. The same chiller systems that serve brewery cellars are appropriate for all fermentation applications.

faqs.q: Do you offer installation services for glycol chillers?
faqs.a: Yes. Chiller installation involves glycol loop connection, electrical hookup, and commissioning. Our team coordinates delivery and can advise on installation requirements and qualified local contractors.

faqs.q: What happens if the chiller I need is not currently in inventory?
faqs.a: Contact us with your required tonnage and configuration. We source specific equipment on request and can typically locate the right chiller within our dealer network within a few weeks.

buyerGuide.title: Calculate Heat Load
buyerGuide.description: Total heat load depends on fermentation vessel volume, ambient temperature, and crash cooling frequency. Undersizing by even 20% creates production constraints.

buyerGuide.title: Confirm Tonnage Range
buyerGuide.description: Match chiller tonnage to your calculated heat load with a 20-30% safety margin. Account for future cellar expansion when sizing.

buyerGuide.title: Review Glycol Loop Design
buyerGuide.description: Confirm the chiller output temperature, flow rate, and glycol concentration match your vessel jacket specifications and existing piping.

buyerGuide.title: Check Electrical Requirements
buyerGuide.description: Large chillers require 3-phase electrical service. Confirm your facility electrical infrastructure before purchasing.

buyerGuide.title: Plan Installation
buyerGuide.description: Chiller location, condensing unit placement, and glycol loop routing all require planning before the unit arrives. Our team can advise on installation requirements.

bestFitBuyers.title: New Cellar Build
bestFitBuyers.description: Building out fermentation capacity and need a chiller sized to your full planned vessel count? We source units to your spec.

bestFitBuyers.title: Existing Chiller Replacement
bestFitBuyers.description: Current chiller failing or undersized for expanded cellar volume? Used production chillers offer immediate availability at below-new cost.

bestFitBuyers.title: Cellar Expansion
bestFitBuyers.description: Adding fermentation volume and your current chiller is at capacity? We source supplemental units sized to your additional load.

bestFitBuyers.title: Distillery or Cidery
bestFitBuyers.description: Glycol chilling applies equally to distillery fermentation and cider production. Our inventory serves all beverage fermentation applications.

equipmentCategories.title: Distilling Equipment
equipmentCategories.h1: Distilling Equipment For Sale: Stills and Systems
equipmentCategories.metaTitle: Distilling Equipment For Sale — Stills & Systems | Beverage Equipment Traders
equipmentCategories.metaDescription: New and used distilling equipment including copper pot stills, column stills, and complete distillery systems. Ships nationwide from San Marcos, CA.
equipmentCategories.description: Copper pot stills, column stills, and complete distillery systems for craft spirits producers. Current inventory includes a 300-gallon copper pot still.
equipmentCategories.bodyContent:
Craft distillery equipment for producers at every stage — from startup operations sourcing first-generation stills through established distilleries liquidating or upgrading. Our inventory includes copper pot stills, column stills, and supporting equipment including proofing tanks, fermenters, and bottling systems suited for spirits production.

The distillery equipment market extends well beyond the craft beverage industry. Food-grade processing, essential oil extraction, water distillation, and industrial applications all use similar still configurations. Our buyer base for distillery equipment is geographically distributed across all 50 states.

Current inventory includes a 300-gallon copper pot still at $75,000 — a standout piece for a craft distillery building out a small-batch program or looking to expand capacity. Pricing reflects current market conditions, equipment condition, manufacturer, and configuration.

Contact us for current distillery equipment availability. We can also assist with sourcing specific still configurations on request.

faqs.q: Do you sell complete distillery setups?
faqs.a: We carry individual stills as well as supporting equipment. Complete multi-vessel distillery configurations are available depending on current inventory.

faqs.q: Can distilling equipment be used for food processing?
faqs.a: Yes. Many still configurations have applications beyond spirits — essential oil extraction, food processing, and industrial uses. Contact us to discuss your specific requirements.

faqs.q: Do you carry column stills as well as pot stills?
faqs.a: Yes. Both copper pot stills and column stills appear in our inventory. Current inventory includes a 300-gallon copper pot still and a Barison continuous column still. Contact us for current availability on specific configurations.

faqs.q: Can you help with TTB compliance documentation for still purchases?
faqs.a: We provide available manufacturer documentation and specifications with every still purchase. TTB permit requirements are the buyer responsibility — we recommend consulting with a TTB compliance specialist before purchasing.

faqs.q: Do you buy distillery equipment from operations that are closing?
faqs.a: Yes. We handle consignment, direct purchase, and auction of distillery equipment from closing operations. Contact us to discuss your equipment set and which liquidation approach best fits your timeline.

buyerGuide.title: Define Still Type
buyerGuide.description: Pot stills produce heavier, more flavorful spirits suited for whiskey and rum. Column stills produce higher-proof neutral spirits. Hybrid stills offer flexibility across both styles.

buyerGuide.title: Determine Capacity
buyerGuide.description: Still capacity is measured in gallons of wash per run. Match still size to your fermentation vessel output and target production volume.

buyerGuide.title: Review Regulatory Requirements
buyerGuide.description: Distillery equipment requires TTB approval and state licensing. Confirm your permit allows the still configuration you are purchasing before committing.

buyerGuide.title: Confirm Utility Requirements
buyerGuide.description: Stills require heat source (steam, direct fire, or electric), cooling water, and condensate handling. Verify your facility infrastructure matches the still requirements.

buyerGuide.title: Plan Installation
buyerGuide.description: Still installation involves utility connections, drainage, and often structural considerations. Our team coordinates delivery and can advise on installation requirements.

bestFitBuyers.title: Craft Distillery Startup
bestFitBuyers.description: Launching a craft spirits program and sourcing your first still? Used production stills offer proven performance at significantly below-new cost.

bestFitBuyers.title: Capacity Expansion
bestFitBuyers.description: Current still is the production bottleneck? Adding a second still or upgrading to larger capacity to meet demand.

bestFitBuyers.title: Brewery Adding Spirits
bestFitBuyers.description: Craft brewery expanding into spirits production? We source stills sized for craft production programs that complement existing brewing infrastructure.

bestFitBuyers.title: Industrial Applications
bestFitBuyers.description: Food processing, essential oil extraction, and industrial distillation applications use similar equipment. We source stills for non-spirits applications as well.

equipmentCategories.title: Kegs & Casks
equipmentCategories.h1: Used Kegs and Cask Kegs For Sale
equipmentCategories.metaTitle: Used Kegs & Cask Kegs For Sale | Beverage Equipment Traders
equipmentCategories.metaDescription: Half barrel kegs, 1/6 BBL kegs, cask kegs, and more. In stock at our San Marcos, CA facility. Ships nationwide.
equipmentCategories.description: Half barrel kegs, sixth barrel kegs, cask kegs, and real ales containers. Multiple sizes in stock with nationwide shipping available.
equipmentCategories.bodyContent:
Kegs and cask containers for production breweries, taprooms, and specialty producers. Our inventory covers the full range of standard and specialty keg formats: half barrel (1/2 BBL), quarter barrel (1/4 BBL), sixth barrel (1/6 BBL), and cask kegs in both pin and firkin sizes.

Cask kegs serve the real ale and cask-conditioned market, as well as producers selling to on-premise accounts that have installed cask tap systems. We currently rank in the top results for cask keg searches, reflecting genuine inventory depth in this niche.

Used kegs from our inventory are sourced from production breweries and arrive with legitimate brewing history. New kegs from select manufacturers are also available. Contact us for volume pricing on bulk keg purchases.

faqs.q: Do you sell cask kegs for real ale production?
faqs.a: Yes. Pin casks and firkins are available for real ale and cask-conditioned production. Contact us for current inventory.

faqs.q: Do you offer volume pricing on bulk keg purchases?
faqs.a: Yes. Pallet and full-truckload quantities are available with volume pricing. Contact us with your required quantity and format for a bulk quote.

faqs.q: Are your used kegs pressure-tested before sale?
faqs.a: Used kegs in our inventory are sourced from production breweries with documented maintenance history. We recommend buyers pressure-test and valve-inspect all used kegs before putting them into service.

faqs.q: What keg valve types do you typically carry?
faqs.a: Sanke D is the US standard and the most common format in our inventory. European Sanke A, S, and G valves appear occasionally. Valve type is noted in listings — confirm compatibility with your accounts before purchasing.

buyerGuide.title: Determine Keg Format
buyerGuide.description: Half barrel (15.5 gal) for standard draft accounts. Sixth barrel for specialty and rotating taps. Cask kegs (pin and firkin) for real ale programs.

buyerGuide.title: Calculate Volume Needed
buyerGuide.description: Match keg inventory to your weekly packaging volume and average account cycle time. Most production breweries maintain 3-4x weekly production volume in keg inventory.

buyerGuide.title: Inspect Condition
buyerGuide.description: Used kegs should be pressure-tested and valve-inspected. Our used keg inventory is sourced from production breweries with known maintenance history.

buyerGuide.title: Confirm Valve Type
buyerGuide.description: Sanke D is the US standard. European Sanke A, S, and G valves require different couplers. Confirm valve compatibility with your accounts before purchasing.

buyerGuide.title: Arrange Delivery
buyerGuide.description: Kegs ship efficiently by pallet. Contact us for volume pricing and freight quotes on bulk orders.

bestFitBuyers.title: New Draft Program
bestFitBuyers.description: Launching draft distribution and need an initial keg inventory? We source matched sets of used half barrels and sixth barrels for startup programs.

bestFitBuyers.title: Expanding Distribution
bestFitBuyers.description: Adding accounts and need to increase keg inventory? Bulk used keg purchases at competitive pricing.

bestFitBuyers.title: Real Ale Program
bestFitBuyers.description: Launching cask-conditioned ales? Pin casks and firkins for real ale programs serving on-premise accounts with cask tap installations.

bestFitBuyers.title: Taproom Only
bestFitBuyers.description: Taproom-focused operation needing sixth barrel kegs for rotating tap handles? Small quantities available alongside bulk orders.

equipmentCategories.title: Boilers
equipmentCategories.h1: Commercial Boilers For Sale: Brewery and Beverage
equipmentCategories.metaTitle: Commercial Boilers For Sale — Brewery & Beverage | Beverage Equipment Traders
equipmentCategories.metaDescription: New and used commercial boilers for brewing. Rite boilers, Lattner boilers, Sussman electric boilers in stock. Ships nationwide.
equipmentCategories.description: Commercial steam boilers for brewery and beverage operations, including Rite, Lattner, and Sussman models in various BTU ratings.
equipmentCategories.bodyContent:
Commercial steam boilers for brewery, distillery, and beverage production. We carry steam boilers from Rite Engineering, Lattner Boiler Company, Sussman Electric Boilers, and other manufacturers suited for direct-steam brewhouse systems and process heating applications.

Boiler selection depends on your steam demand, facility utility infrastructure, and whether you are running a direct-fire or steam-heated brewhouse. Steam boilers require licensed installation and are subject to local utility and pressure vessel regulations — we recommend buyers verify local requirements before purchase.

Used boilers in our inventory are sourced from working production facilities. Condition documentation, nameplate specs, and operational history are provided where available. New Sussman electric boilers represent a code-compliant, lower-installation-complexity option for facilities where natural gas or steam systems are constrained.

faqs.q: Do I need a licensed installer for a commercial boiler?
faqs.a: Yes. Commercial steam boilers are pressure vessels subject to state and local codes. Installation must be performed by licensed professionals. We can provide referrals to qualified installers.

faqs.q: What boiler size do I need for my brewhouse?
faqs.a: Boiler sizing depends on your brewhouse heating requirements and brew schedule. Most brewhouse manufacturers specify minimum BTU requirements — confirm with your system manufacturer before purchasing a boiler.

faqs.q: Do you carry electric boilers as an alternative to gas?
faqs.a: Yes. Sussman electric boilers are available and represent a code-simpler installation for facilities where natural gas is constrained or unavailable. Higher operating cost than gas but lower installation complexity.

faqs.q: Can boilers from brewery liquidations be relocated and reinstalled?
faqs.a: Yes. Used boilers can be relocated with proper permitting and licensed installation. We coordinate rigging and transport — buyers are responsible for local permits and licensed installation at the new facility.

buyerGuide.title: Calculate Steam Demand
buyerGuide.description: Steam demand is driven by brewhouse heating requirements and process timing. Work with your brewhouse manufacturer or an engineer to confirm BTU requirements.

buyerGuide.title: Choose Fuel Type
buyerGuide.description: Natural gas is standard for most facilities. Electric boilers (Sussman) are code-simpler but higher operating cost. Confirm your facility utility availability.

buyerGuide.title: Verify Local Codes
buyerGuide.description: Pressure vessel installation requires permits and licensed installers in most jurisdictions. Verify local requirements before purchasing.

buyerGuide.title: Review Condition Documentation
buyerGuide.description: Used boilers should have current inspection certification and maintenance records. Our listings include available documentation on condition and service history.

buyerGuide.title: Coordinate Licensed Installation
buyerGuide.description: Boiler installation requires licensed plumbers and engineers. We can provide referrals to qualified installers familiar with brewery applications.

bestFitBuyers.title: Steam-Heated Brewhouse
bestFitBuyers.description: Running or building a steam-heated brewhouse and need a matched boiler? We source boilers to your BTU requirements.

bestFitBuyers.title: Boiler Replacement
bestFitBuyers.description: Existing boiler failing or at end of service life? Used production boilers offer immediate availability at below-replacement cost.

bestFitBuyers.title: Distillery Steam System
bestFitBuyers.description: Steam-heated stills and process equipment require the same boiler infrastructure as brewhouses. We source boilers for distillery applications.

bestFitBuyers.title: Food Processing
bestFitBuyers.description: Commercial steam boilers serve food processing, dairy, and beverage production applications beyond brewing. Contact us to discuss your specific requirements.

equipmentCategories.title: Cold Rooms
equipmentCategories.h1: Cold Rooms and Cold Boxes For Brewing
equipmentCategories.metaTitle: Cold Rooms & Cold Boxes For Sale — Brewing & Beverage | Beverage Equipment Traders
equipmentCategories.metaDescription: Used and new cold rooms, walk-in coolers, and cold boxes for brewing and beverage operations. In stock in San Marcos, CA. Ships nationwide.
equipmentCategories.description: Walk-in coolers, cold rooms, and cold boxes for temperature-controlled storage in brewing and beverage production facilities.
equipmentCategories.bodyContent:
Cold storage for breweries, beverage producers, and food processing operations. Walk-in coolers, cold rooms, and modular cold boxes in various configurations — from compact taproom cold storage through large-format production coolers for high-volume packaging facilities.

Cold room configuration involves floor dimensions, door placement, refrigeration unit type (remote vs. self-contained), and target temperature range. Brewery cold storage typically operates between 34°F and 38°F for bright beer storage and lagering. Fermentation cold rooms require broader temperature control ranges including crash cooling capability.

Used cold room panels and refrigeration units from our inventory are typically sourced from food and beverage facility liquidations. Condition includes panel integrity, gasket condition, and refrigeration unit operational status. Contact us for current cold room inventory and custom configuration options.

faqs.q: Can you configure a cold room to specific dimensions?
faqs.a: Panel-based cold rooms can often be reconfigured to different dimensions. Contact us with your floor plan and we can advise on what is possible with available inventory.

faqs.q: What temperature ranges do your cold rooms support?
faqs.a: Most cold rooms in our inventory are configured for 34 to 38 degrees F — standard bright beer and serving temperature. Units supporting wider ranges including crash cooling to 32 degrees F are noted in listings.

faqs.q: Do you handle delivery and installation of cold rooms?
faqs.a: Yes. Cold room installation involves floor preparation, electrical, and often drain work. Our team coordinates delivery and can advise on installation requirements and qualified local contractors.

faqs.q: Can cold rooms from brewery liquidations be used for food storage?
faqs.a: Yes. Walk-in coolers from brewery and beverage liquidations are food-grade and appropriate for produce, dairy, and food storage applications. Panel construction and refrigeration type are noted in all listings.

buyerGuide.title: Define Temperature Range
buyerGuide.description: Bright beer and serving cold rooms operate at 34-38°F. Fermentation rooms require wider range including crash cooling to 32°F. Confirm your temperature requirements before sizing refrigeration.

buyerGuide.title: Calculate Square Footage
buyerGuide.description: Size cold room to your peak package inventory — typically 2-3 weeks of production for distribution operations. Add 20% margin for operational flexibility.

buyerGuide.title: Choose Refrigeration Type
buyerGuide.description: Self-contained units are simpler to install. Remote condensing units are quieter and more efficient for larger rooms. Confirm your facility layout accommodates your choice.

buyerGuide.title: Review Panel Condition
buyerGuide.description: Insulated panels should be free of moisture infiltration and structural damage. Gaskets and door hardware are wear items — confirm their condition in our listings.

buyerGuide.title: Plan Installation
buyerGuide.description: Cold room installation requires floor preparation, electrical, and often drain work. Our team can advise on installation requirements and coordinate delivery.

bestFitBuyers.title: Taproom Cold Storage
bestFitBuyers.description: Adding cold storage to a taproom operation for keg and package inventory? Compact walk-in units sized for taproom volume.

bestFitBuyers.title: Production Brewery
bestFitBuyers.description: Scaling production and need larger cold storage for bright beer and packaged product? Production-scale cold rooms from brewery liquidations.

bestFitBuyers.title: Distribution Operation
bestFitBuyers.description: Building out distribution cold storage for packaged product inventory? Large-format cold rooms sized for distribution volume.

bestFitBuyers.title: Food Processing
bestFitBuyers.description: Cold rooms from brewery and beverage liquidations serve food processing, dairy, and produce storage applications. Same panel systems, different contents.
